description: Running commands after a `cd` that may have failed, so subsequent operations execute in the wrong directory.
body: Failure mode: a script does `cd /some/dir` then `rm -rf ./build`, but if the cd failed (missing dir, typo) it stays in the original directory and the rm targets the wrong tree. Detection signals: files deleted or created in an unexpected location; 'No such file or directory' from cd ignored; operations affect the repo root instead of a subdir. Do not chain destructive commands after an unchecked cd. Safer fallback: use `cd /some/dir || exit 1` (or `cd ... && cmd`), prefer absolute paths, and guard recursive deletes with an explicit, verified path; confirm the working directory with pwd before destructive steps.
